name: India
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **INDIAN MARKETS REPRICED SHARPLY — Sensex closed May 11 at 76,015.28 (-1,312.91 pts, -1.70%) = LARGEST SINGLE-DAY POINT DROP OF CYCLE; YTD drop crossed 10,000 points; Nifty 23,815.85 (-360.30 pts, -1.49%); Bank Nifty 54,439.90 (-870.65) before V-shape May 12 recovery to 56,565.70 (+0.85%); USD/INR ₹95.0930 May 11 → ₹95.54 May 12 (intraday low ₹95.32 record) — ₹95 STRUCTURAL TRIGGER RE-BREACHED, CLEANLY BROKE RBI'S ₹95.50 DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD; FII single-day net sell ₹8,437.56 cr May 11 (highest since April 24); YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR (already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr).** Market dossier C2 assessment: India deployment timing being pushed OUT, not forward, by today's tape. The cascade is now self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up → debt-FPI also out → more INR pressure. **B8 PM MODI HYDERABAD ADDRESS MAY 10-11: seven-appeal package — (i) reduce fuel consumption (work from home), (ii) defer foreign travel for one year, (iii) defer gold purchases (incl. weddings) for one year, (iv) reduce edible oil consumption, (v) cut fertilizer ~50% (mixed organic), (vi) push swadeshi, (vii) general consumption restraint** — first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle, targeting two specific FX pressure points (gold imports ~$50bn/yr drain + crude import bill). Al Jazeera framed 'Iran war effect: Why is Modi asking Indians to avoid foreign trips, gold?' Indian media (Business Standard, Livemint, Indian Express) received as sober/serious, NOT panic. MEA separately issued 'deeply concerned' statement urging restraint and calling for 'unimpeded' Hormuz trade. **B16 PM Modi 5-nation tour May 15-20: UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Italy** — explicit energy/defence/trade diversification; UAE leg materially complicated by WSJ Lavan revelation (B6). **B13 INDIA FY27 GDP DOWNGRADE TO 6.2% FROM 6.7%** (global brokerage May 11; 'historically large energy shock with asymmetric macro risks'). **RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn to $690.69bn week ended May 1 (sharp drawdown, active intervention); reserves down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak of $728.49bn.** India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95% (auction-anchored despite crude bid). Sectoral: PSU OMCs (BPCL -2.34%, HPCL -2.65%, IOC -2.45% May 11) face Q1 FY27 losses large enough to wipe full-year FY26 profits per brokerage flags; Reliance May 12 intraday +1.99% (refining margin tailwind diverges from OMC marketing-margin compression); Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday (rupee weakness usually a tailwind but US-growth fears dominating); Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD (defensive bid intact). **STRUCTURAL STORY: US (S&P 500 fresh record, NASDAQ +14.7% 1M on AI) and Korea (KOSPI +33% 1M on AI semis) running their own cycles while India takes the whole brunt of the energy-cascade — worst import-FII-currency triple-vulnerability of major economies.** Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) INR retracement to ₹93 handle. None imminent. Goldman 2026 Brent average $85 implicitly requires Q4 average $71 — bullish case is 'back half of year.' Bearish case: no deal + kinetic escalation = INR ₹96-97 + Nifty toward 22,000.

current.price: Nifty 23,578.00 May 12 close (markets dossier Section A; Nifty May 11 close 23,815.85, recovered May 12); Sensex 75,191.36 May 12 close (May 11 close 76,015.28); Bank Nifty 53,914.00 May 12 close (V-shape recovery from 54,439.90 May 11 to 56,565.70 intraday); USD-INR ₹95.54 (intraday record May 12); India 10Y 6.96-6.99%
current.delta_1d: Nifty -1.0% May 12 (May 11 -1.49%); Sensex -1.1% May 12 (May 11 -1.70% largest single-day point drop of cycle); Bank Nifty -1.0% May 12 (May 11 -1.6%); USD/INR +1.2% May 12 (May 11 -0.7% breach of ₹95); FII single-day sell ₹8,437.56 cr May 11
current.delta_1m: Nifty -0.8%; Sensex -1.9%; Bank Nifty -1.7%; USD/INR +1.1%; YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 lakh cr (exceeds full-year 2025 by ₹40k cr)
current.delta_3m: Nifty -8.6%; Sensex -10.1%; Bank Nifty -11.2%; USD/INR +5.3%; Cumulative Sensex drop crossed 10,000 points YTD
current.deployment_context: WORST POSITIONING OF MAJOR ECONOMIES. India takes whole brunt of energy-cascade pressure — worst import-FII-currency triple-vulnerability. PSU OMC Q1 FY27 losses could wipe full-year FY26 profits per brokerage flags. RBI reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 = active intervention not stopping. Modi seven-appeal address signals FX seriousness. FY27 GDP cut 6.7%→6.2%. US-vs-India decoupling is STRUCTURAL story of the day. Deployment requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII positive + INR ₹93 — none imminent. Bearish scenario: ₹96-97 INR + Nifty 22,000 if no deal + kinetic escalation.

active_triggers.condition: Rupee crosses ₹95/USD — DEACTIVATED on ceasefire. Current ₹92.55. WATCHING.
India announces fuel-price pass-through OR Iran-rupee oil track restart via Chabahar within 30 days (by 2026-05-27)

RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn to $690.69bn week ended May 1 (sharp drawdown, evidence active intervention; down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak $728.49bn). **PM MODI HYDERABAD ADDRESS MAY 10-11: seven appeals — defer gold purchases (incl. weddings) for 1 year + defer foreign travel for 1 year + cut fertilizer ~50% + reduce edible oil + reduce fuel + swadeshi + consumption restraint** = first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle, targets gold imports ($50bn/yr drain) + crude bill. Al Jazeera framed 'Iran war effect.' MEA 'deeply concerned' statement urging restraint + 'unimpeded' Hormuz trade. **PM MODI 5-NATION TOUR MAY 15-20: UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Italy** — energy/defence/trade diversification (UAE leg complicated by WSJ Lavan revelation). **FY27 GDP DOWNGRADE TO 6.2% FROM 6.7%** (global brokerage; 'historically large energy shock'). Sectoral: PSU OMCs (BPCL -2.34%, HPCL -2.65%, IOC -2.45% May 11) face Q1 FY27 losses large enough to wipe full-year FY26 profits per brokerage flags; Reliance May 12 intraday +1.99% (refining margin tailwind); Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday (US-growth fears overwhelm rupee tailwind); Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD (defensive bid intact). **STRUCTURAL STORY: US S&P 500 fresh record + NASDAQ +14.7% 1M on AI; KOSPI +33% 1M on AI semis; India takes whole brunt — worst import-FII-currency triple-vulnerability of major economies.**

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — INDIAN MARKETS CLOSED; FRIDAY MAY 8 CLOSE STILL OPERATIVE: Nifty 24,176.15 (-0.6%); Sensex 77,328.19 (-0.7%); Bank Nifty 55,310.55 (-1.3% LEADING). FII outflows ₹340.89 cr May 8; ₹1.92 LAKH CR YTD CY26 already exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr). FPI ownership ~16%, lowest in 2 decades. INR ₹94.47 stable; India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0%. **No fresh Indian-source diplomatic engagement piece today. The intelligence relevant for India today is silence: no MEA statement, no PM Modi statement, no Jaishankar statement, no public India position on Qatar mediator emergence, the bulk carrier strike near Doha, the Bahrain detentions, or the Pezeshkian meeting claim. India's 'strategic autonomy' posture continues; the absence is the position.** Eight-million-strong Indian Gulf diaspora exposure factor remains active.
sources: Searched MEA / PMO / EAM Twitter handles — CONFIRMED ABSENCE of statement

recent_signals.headline: INDIAN MARKETS — Nifty 24,360 (+0.12%) / Sensex 77,949 (-0.01%); Gift Nifty +0.67% pre-open. USD/INR ₹94.74 (-0.5%) pulled back from ₹95.36 record on Brent collapse + DXY weakness. India 10Y ~6.99% (-3-5bps). FII outflow May 5 ₹3,621 cr; week net ₹13,771 cr selling vs DII ₹11,585 cr buying (absorption 84%, lower than prior ~90%). [CORRECTED 2026-05-08: prior version of this signal incorrectly conflated a 2025 Iran-India Joint Commission (Araghchi-Jaishankar) as a current 2026 event. The 20th JCM took place in May 2025; no equivalent meeting occurred on 2026-05-07. Year-stale-source error caught by post-publication user review.]
